items.title: June 5, 2002: This slam dunk by Kobe Bryant during the Lakers' 99-94 win over New Jersey in Game 1 of the NBA Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 5, 2002: This slam dunk by Kobe Bryant during the Lakers' 99-94 win over New Jersey in Game 1 of the NBA Finals.

Bryant finished with 22 PTS/6 AST and Shaquille O'Neal had 36 PTS/16 REB/4 BLK to lead Los Angeles. Jason Kidd had 23 PTS/10 REB/10 AST/3 STL for the Nets.

items.title: June 5, 1986: The Celtics and Rockets brawl during Game 5 of the NBA Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 5, 1986: The Celtics and Rockets brawl during Game 5 of the NBA Finals.

Houston's Ralph Sampson and Boston's Jerry Sichting were ejected. Sampson was fined $5,000. Sichting, Dennis Johnson and Akeem Olajuwon were each fined $1,500, and 13 other players were fined $500 each.

items.title: Julius Randle’s offensive play type efficiency in this years playoffs.
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
Julius Randle’s offensive play type efficiency in this years playoffs.

Points Per Possession 

• Average efficiency on post ups
• Below average on isolations
• Bottom of the league efficiency in everything else 

Tool: courtsketch.com/player

images.alt:
Bubble chart titled “Julius Randle PS : 2026” showing play type frequency and success in scoring, measured by points per possession (PPP) on the y-axis as a percentile ranking. Bubble size represents frequency. Color scale runs from orange (0th percentile) to purple (100th percentile).

Seven play types are shown along the x-axis:

Cut (4.2%) — PPP 1.2, 0th percentile, small orange bubble
Isolation (14.3%) — PPP 0.88, ~35th percentile, medium pink bubble
Putback (9.7%) — PPP 1.04, 0th percentile, small orange bubble
Post-Up (12.2%) — PPP 0.93, ~50th percentile, medium pink-purple bubble
Handler (9.7%) — PPP 0.39, 0th percentile, small orange bubble
Spot (21.9%) — PPP 0.77, ~8th percentile, largest bubble on the chart, orange
Transition (14.8%) — PPP 0.80, ~10th percentile, large orange bubble

items.title: I still think Hammond is fake leverage, that site is a shithole (sorry), George would ruin the family legacy forever by moving the team there. Soldier Field is fine. The Bears don't need a new stadium and can't afford to pay for one, so they might as well get comfortable renting.
items.author: Ricky O'Donnell
items.body_excerpt: I still think Hammond is fake leverage, that site is a shithole (sorry), George would ruin the family legacy forever by moving the team there. Soldier Field is fine. The Bears don't need a new stadium and can't afford to pay for one, so they might as well get comfortable renting.
